behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing; missing fields return one clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff gate: validation must prevent calls into insurance/triage/scheduling when fields are missing. A unit test of field-check logic alone can miss downstream invocation bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake through the orchestration layer with missing `name` and `insurance_id`; assert returned error lists both fields and assert downstream modules (insurance API client, triage, scheduler) are not invoked.

behaviors.description: Date-of-birth validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This is pure input-validation logic in one boundary with no required I/O or module interaction to expose the target failure.
behaviors.test_description: Test DOB validator with representative invalid values (future date, 151+ years old, malformed strings) and one valid boundary case; assert specific validation errors.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID/plan code and correctly interprets `eligible`/`not eligible`/`pending`.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often appears at the HTTP contract boundary (serialization/field names/status mapping). Mocked clients can hide request-shape and protocol issues.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real payer sandbox (or real HTTP contract endpoint), submit known cases and verify outbound payload contains exact patient ID/plan code and response mapping is correct for all three statuses.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is set to `pending` and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: The behavior depends on real network failure modes/timeouts and continuation through the live orchestration path; mocks can miss retry/timeout/socket behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake with payer endpoint intentionally unreachable (connection refused/timeout) and verify eligibility becomes `pending`, pipeline continues to scheduling/confirmation, and no hard failure is returned.

behaviors.description: AI triage assigns high urgency to emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness is about model judgment quality, not deterministic code wiring. Real model outputs can vary and must be validated on real calls.
behaviors.test_description: Execute repeated real triage model calls on curated emergency symptom cases and assert high-urgency classification rate meets threshold (e.g., >=95%) with drift tolerance.

behaviors.description: Triage returns a clinician-reviewable natural-language explanation, not only a numeric score.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real generated language quality/format compliance from the AI model; deterministic mocks cannot catch explanation adequacy failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real triage model for representative symptom inputs and assert response includes non-empty explanation text meeting minimal clinical readability/structure checks plus urgency value.

behaviors.description: Scheduling picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (high=same day, medium<=48h).
behaviors.justification: Failure occurs where scheduling logic interacts with shared calendar persistence and availability queries. Pure unit tests can miss DB ordering/query issues.
behaviors.test_description: With a local calendar DB fixture containing occupied/free slots, run scheduler for high and medium urgency and assert chosen slot is earliest valid one within SLA window.

behaviors.description: Scheduling prevents double-booking when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: The target failure is concurrent interaction with shared DB state (locking/transaction constraints) across modules, not single-function logic.
behaviors.test_description: Launch two concurrent intake/scheduling requests targeting same candidate slot against a real local DB with production transaction settings; assert only one booking succeeds and the other is re-assigned/rejected without duplicate row.

behaviors.description: Confirmation includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-specific preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: Risk is in composing data from scheduling/provider/visit-type modules into outbound message; unit template checks alone can miss missing-field wiring issues.
behaviors.test_description: Run confirmation generation from persisted appointment + visit type records and assert message body contains all required fields and correct prep instructions for each visit type.

behaviors.description: End-to-end intake pipeline (validate → insurance → triage → schedule → confirm) completes for a real submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end and includes a real AI triage step whose output affects downstream flow; minimum infrastructure must include the live model call plus real external/system interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a realistic patient intake through the production-like pipeline using real payer API path, real triage model, and real scheduling DB; assert successful completion with final confirmation artifact and no human override.
